name: Return to the Aznaitin hill - circular in the Sierra Mágina Natural Park
name_original: Vuelta al cerro de Aznaitin - circular por el Parque Natural de Sierra Mágina
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Albanchez De Mágina are situated within the rugged, mountainous terrain of the Sierra Mágina Natural Park in Jaén, Spain. This region is characterized by precipitous cliffs, high summits, and diverse Mediterranean forests of holm oaks and gall oaks. Cyclists can navigate challenging elevations and experience the "sea of olive trees" that defines the Jaén province. The landscape also features numerous springs and natural water sources, contributing to its varied natural beauty. Best touring cycling routes around Albanchez De Mágina The most popular touring cycling route is Return to the Aznaitin hill - circular in the Sierra Mágina Natural Park, a 25.1 miles (40.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 26 minutes to complete. This route offers a challenging ascent within the natural park. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Fuente Mivararis – Fuente del Puerco loop from Torres, a difficult 23.9 miles (38.5 km) path. This loop explores the mountainous terrain and natural features around Torres. Local touring cyclists also love the Views of Cerro de la Carluca – Cueva del Agua de Bedmar loop from Bedmar y Garcíez, a 25.1 miles (40.3 km) trail leading through scenic viewpoints and natural formations, often completed in about 4 hours 9 minutes. Touring cycling around Albanchez De Mágina is defined by rugged mountainous terrain, the dramatic peaks of the Sierra Mágina Natural Park, and extensive olive groves. The available routes are generally challenging, catering to experienced touring cyclists. The routes in Albanchez De Mágina are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 3 reviews. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Albanchez De Mágina's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of touring cycling routes around Albanchez De Mágina?
A: The touring cycling routes in this region are generally challenging, catering to experienced cyclists. All 16 available routes are classified as difficult, featuring significant elevation gains and rugged mountainous terrain within the Sierra Mágina Natural Park.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. For example, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995540962/return-to-the-aznaitin-hill-circular-in-the-sierra-magina-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to the Aznaitin hill - circular in the Sierra Mágina Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a challenging ascent. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17268714" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Mivararis – Fuente del Puerco loop from Torres&lt;/a&gt;, exploring the natural features around Torres.

Q: What kind of landscapes and natural features can I expect to see while cycling?
A: Cyclists will encounter rugged, mountainous terrain, precipitous cliffs, and high summits within the Sierra Mágina Natural Park. The routes often pass through diverse Mediterranean forests of holm oaks and gall oaks. You'll also experience stunning vistas of the 'sea of olive trees' characteristic of Jaén province, along with numerous springs and natural water sources.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region offers several impressive landmarks and viewpoints. You can find routes that pass by historical fortifications and castles, reflecting the area's past. For panoramic views, consider routes that offer sights of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4754178" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Cerro de la Carluca&lt;/a&gt;, or even the majestic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4444835" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mágina Summit (2,167 m)&lt;/a&gt;, the highest peak in Jaén province.

Q: What natural water features can be found in the area?
A: The Sierra Mágina Natural Park is rich in water sources. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5198955" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Cuadros River&lt;/a&gt;, also known as 'El Sistillo,' which is a highlight of the 'Ruta del Adelfal'. The region also features the spectacular Caldera del Tío Lobo with its 50-meter waterfall and pools, and the Paraje de Hútar, noted for its pools and waterfalls at the source of the Hútar River.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Albanchez De Mágina?
A: Given the challenging and mountainous terrain, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for touring cycling in Albanchez De Mágina. The weather is milder, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter, making for more comfortable rides.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Albanchez De Mágina?
A: The touring cycling routes around Albanchez De Mágina are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from multiple reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging nature of the routes, the stunning panoramic views, and the immersive experience within the rugged Sierra Mágina Natural Park.

Q: Are there any routes that explore historical sites?
A: Yes, the historical significance of Sierra Mágina as a former border region means that some cycling routes may pass by ancient fortifications and castles. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19380304" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santiago el Mayor – Keep Tower loop from Jimena&lt;/a&gt; offers a glimpse into the region's past.

Q: Can I expect to see any wildlife while cycling?
A: The Sierra Mágina Natural Park is known for its rich biodiversity. You might spot various wildlife, including golden eagles (the park has one of the highest concentrations in Europe), Spanish ibex, wild boar, and numerous smaller mammals and birds, especially in the Mediterranean forests.

Q: Are there any shorter touring cycling routes for a quicker ride?
A: While all routes are classified as difficult, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27625667" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Cuadros River – Cueva del Agua de Bedmar loop from Bedmar y Garcíez&lt;/a&gt; is one of the shorter options, at approximately 24.0 km. It still presents a significant challenge with considerable elevation gain but can be completed in a shorter duration compared to other routes.

Q: What are the typical elevation changes on these routes?
A: The routes in Albanchez De Mágina feature significant elevation changes, reflecting the mountainous terrain. For example, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995540962/return-to-the-aznaitin-hill-circular-in-the-sierra-magina-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to the Aznaitin hill&lt;/a&gt; involve over 1000 meters of ascent, making them demanding for even experienced touring cyclists.
tips: The Torreón de Cuadros is a cylindrical tower located in Bedmar y Garcíeza. This tower has a diameter of 6.37 meters and a height of 12 meters, with walls 1.6 meters thick. It was built on an earlier structure, possibly of Moorish origin, and is mentioned in a document by Alfonso X from 1260.

Cerro Aznatín de Albanchez is a prominent mountain peak with an elevation of approximately 1,732 metres (5,700 ft), located in the municipality of Albanchez de Mágina in the province of Jaén, Spain. It is one of the three "Aznatín" peaks in the region and is known for its striking appearance, especially when viewed from the east or the Guadalquivir Valley.

Jimena is a small town in the south of the province, on the northern side of the Sierra Mágina, located in a landscape of olive groves, fruit trees, allotments and sierra. The town is particularly known for its figs and brebas. The municipality is divided into two very different areas: there is the mountainous south-eastern area that is part of the Sierra Mágina Natural Park, and the rest of the municipality is agricultural land dedicated to olives.

The Parque Natural de Sierra Mágina is a spectacular natural area in the province of Jaén, between Jaén and Granada. The park covers approximately 19,900 hectares, lies at an altitude of between 700 and 2,167 metres above sea level and is known for its rugged limestone mountains, deep gorges and picturesque valleys.
